evals.prompt: Set up a local development environment for a NestJS + React + PostgreSQL + Redis project. Need: docker-compose for services, .env configuration, database seeding, and a 'make dev' one-command startup.
evals.expected_output: docker-compose.yml, .env.example, seed script, Makefile with dev target

assertions.name: docker_compose
assertions.description: docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL, Redis, and app services

assertions.name: env_example
assertions.description: .env.example with all required variables (placeholder values)

assertions.name: seed_script
assertions.description: Database seed script for development data

assertions.name: one_command
assertions.description: Single command (make dev or npm run dev) starts everything

assertions.name: health_checks
assertions.description: Services have health checks for dependency ordering

evals.prompt: Configure environment variables for our NestJS app across 3 environments: development (local), staging (AWS), and production (AWS). Need: validation at startup, type-safe config, and no secrets in code.
evals.expected_output: ConfigModule with Joi/Zod validation, typed config, .env files per environment

assertions.name: config_module
assertions.description: NestJS ConfigModule with validation schema

assertions.name: type_safe
assertions.description: Configuration accessed via typed service, not raw process.env

assertions.name: validation
assertions.description: App fails fast on startup if required vars are missing

assertions.name: per_environment
assertions.description: Different values for dev/staging/prod documented

assertions.name: secrets_separate
assertions.description: Secrets not in .env.example, documented as 'set in CI' or 'set in AWS'

evals.prompt: Set up pre-commit hooks for our project: ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript type check, and commit message validation (conventional commits). Use husky + lint-staged.
evals.expected_output: husky + lint-staged configuration with all 4 checks

assertions.name: husky_installed
assertions.description: husky configured in package.json prepare script

assertions.name: lint_staged
assertions.description: lint-staged runs ESLint and Prettier on staged files

assertions.name: type_check
assertions.description: TypeScript type checking hook

assertions.name: commit_msg
assertions.description: commitlint or equivalent for conventional commit validation

assertions.name: package_scripts
assertions.description: Install scripts in package.json for team setup

evals.prompt: Our new developer can't get the project running. They're on macOS, need Node 20, Python 3.11, PostgreSQL 15, and Redis. Create a setup script that installs everything and verifies it works.
evals.expected_output: Setup script checking/installing prerequisites, creating .env, running migrations, and verifying

assertions.name: prerequisite_check
assertions.description: Checks for Node, Python, PostgreSQL, Redis versions

assertions.name: install_guidance
assertions.description: Installs missing tools (Homebrew for macOS)

assertions.name: env_creation
assertions.description: Creates .env from .env.example

assertions.name: migration_run
assertions.description: Runs database migrations

assertions.name: verification
assertions.description: Runs a smoke test to verify everything works

evals.prompt: Add a new service (Python AI service) to our existing Docker Compose development setup. It needs to connect to the same PostgreSQL and Redis instances. The AI service uses Python 3.12 and needs the OpenAI SDK.
evals.expected_output: Updated docker-compose with new AI service, shared network, and Python dependencies

assertions.name: new_service
assertions.description: AI service added to docker-compose.yml

assertions.name: shared_network
assertions.description: AI service on the same Docker network as other services

assertions.name: database_access
assertions.description: AI service can reach PostgreSQL and Redis

assertions.name: python_deps
assertions.description: Python Dockerfile or requirements with OpenAI SDK

assertions.name: env_vars
assertions.description: AI service has its own environment variables including OPENAI_API_KEY placeholder
